title: Exponentiation by Squaring (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the base case is wrong, base the overall correctness marks on whether the recursive steps are otherwise consistent.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is expo(long a, long b, long c); the Python function is expo(a, b, c). Both are correct.
The use of 'import' and '**' (Python power operator) is not allowed. Penalise in point 5 if either appears in the submission.
The algorithm must run in O(log b) recursive depth. Iterative implementations using the same even/odd case split are also acceptable.

rubric_points.name: Base case: b == 0
rubric_points.target: When b == 0, the function returns 1 % c (handles termination and the edge case c == 1).

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No base case is present; the recursion never terminates or returns an incorrect constant (e.g., always returns 0 or 1 regardless of c).

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A base case is present but incorrect: for example, returns 1 without applying % c (fails when c == 1), or the base condition is b == 1 instead of b == 0, causing incorrect results for b == 0.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns 1 % c when b == 0, correctly terminating the recursion and handling the edge case where c == 1.

rubric_points.name: Parity detection: odd vs even b
rubric_points.target: The algorithm correctly distinguishes odd b from even b using b % 2 (or equivalent).

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No parity check is present; odd and even b are treated identically, so only one recursive case is ever applied.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A parity check is present but the odd and even cases are swapped (e.g., b % 2 == 0 is treated as the odd case), producing incorrect results for all non-trivial inputs.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: b % 2 != 0 (or b % 2 == 1) correctly identifies odd b; b % 2 == 0 correctly identifies even b. The two cases lead to different recursive branches.

rubric_points.name: Odd case: reduce by 1 and multiply
rubric_points.target: When b is odd, the function returns (a * expo(a, b-1, c)) % c.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: The odd case is absent, or reduces b by a value other than 1, or does not multiply by a.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Reduces b by 1 and recurses correctly, but does not multiply by a (e.g., returns expo(a, b-1, c) directly), or does not apply % c to the result.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns (a * expo(a, b-1, c)) % c, correctly factoring out one a and reducing the exponent by 1.

rubric_points.name: Even case: halve and square
rubric_points.target: When b is even, the function computes tmp = expo(a, b/2, c) and returns (tmp * tmp) % c.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: The even case is absent, or does not halve b, or does not square the recursive result (e.g., just returns expo(a, b/2, c) without squaring).

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Halves b and recurses, but squares incorrectly (e.g., multiplies by a instead of by itself, or calls expo twice with b/2 which is correct but doubles the recursive work to O(b)), or does not apply % c to the squared result.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Computes tmp = expo(a, b//2, c) once (using integer division), then returns (tmp * tmp) % c, achieving O(log b) depth and correctly squaring the half-power result.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: expo(a, b, c) returns the correct value of a^b mod c for all valid inputs, without using 'import' or '**'.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Returns incorrect results for most inputs, or contains 'import' or '**' in the submission.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct results for small or simple inputs (e.g., b=0, b=1, small a and b) but fails for large b due to integer overflow, missing modulo steps, or incorrect recursion depth.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns a^b mod c correctly for all valid inputs; 'import' and '**' are absent; modulo is applied at each recursive step to keep intermediate values bounded.
